simulate_evoked.description:
.. note:: No projections from ``info`` will be present in the
          output ``evoked``. You can use e.g.
          :func:`evoked.add_proj <mne.Evoked.add_proj>` or
          :func:`evoked.set_eeg_reference <mne.Evoked.set_eeg_reference>`
          to add them afterward as necessary.

trans.description:
Either a transformation filename (usually made using mne_analyze)
or an info dict (usually opened using read_trans()).
If string, an ending of ``.fif`` or ``.fif.gz`` will be assumed to
be in FIF format, any other ending will be assumed to be a text
file with a 4x4 transformation matrix (like the ``--trans`` MNE-C
option). If trans is None, an identity transform will be used.

mindist.description:
Minimum distance between sources and the inner skull boundary
to use during forward calculation.

interp.description:
Either ``'hann'``, ``'cos2'`` (default), ``'linear'``, or ``'zero'``, the type of
forward-solution interpolation to use between forward solutions
at different head positions.

n_jobs.description:
The number of jobs to run in parallel. If ``-1``, it is set
to the number of CPU cores. Requires the :mod:`joblib` package.
``None`` (default) is a marker for 'unset' that will be interpreted
as ``n_jobs=1`` (sequential execution) unless the call is performed under
a :class:`joblib:joblib.parallel_config` context manager that sets another
value for ``n_jobs``.

use_cps.description:
Whether to use cortical patch statistics to define normal orientations for
surfaces (default True).

first_samp.description:
The first_samp property in the output Raw instance.

.. versionadded:: 0.18

max_iter.description:
The maximum number of STC iterations to allow.
This is a sanity parameter to prevent accidental blowups.

.. versionadded:: 0.18

verbose.description:
Control verbosity of the logging output. If ``None``, use the default
verbosity level. See the :ref:`logging documentation <tut-logging>` and
:func:`mne.verbose` for details. Should only be passed as a keyword
argument.

simulate_raw.description:
Head movements can optionally be simulated using the ``head_pos``
parameter.

allow_overlap.description:
Allow overlapping labels or not. Default value is False.

.. versionadded:: 0.18

simulate_stc.description:
This function generates a source estimate with extended sources by
filling the labels with the waveforms given in stc_data.

location.description:
The label location to choose. Can be ``'random'`` (default) or
``'center'`` to use :func:`mne.Label.center_of_mass`. Note that for
``'center'`` mode the label values are used as weights.

.. versionadded:: 0.13

subject.description:
The subject the label is defined for.
Only used with ``location='center'``.

.. versionadded:: 0.13

surf.description:
The surface to use for Euclidean distance center of mass
finding. The default here is "sphere", which finds the center
of mass on the spherical surface to help avoid potential issues
with cortical folding.

.. versionadded:: 0.13

simulate_sparse_stc.description:
This function randomly selects ``n_dipoles`` vertices in the whole
cortex or one single vertex (randomly in or in the center of) each
label if ``labels is not None``. It uses ``data_fun`` to generate
waveforms for each vertex.

location.description:
The label location to choose. Can be 'random' (default) or 'center'
to use :func:`mne.Label.center_of_mass` (restricting to vertices
both in the label and in the source space). Note that for 'center'
mode the label values are used as weights.

.. versionadded:: 0.13

subject.description:
The subject the label is defined for.
Only used with ``location='center'``.

.. versionadded:: 0.13

surf.description:
The surface to use for Euclidean distance center of mass
finding. The default here is "sphere", which finds the center
of mass on the spherical surface to help avoid potential issues
with cortical folding.

.. versionadded:: 0.13

select_source_in_label.description: Select source positions using a label.

tstep.description: Time step between successive samples in data. Default is 0.001 s.

duration.description:
Time interval during which the simulation takes place in seconds.
If None, it is computed using existing events and waveform lengths.

first_samp.description:
First sample from which the simulation takes place, as an integer.
Comparable to the :term:`first_samp` property of `~mne.io.Raw` objects.
Default is 0.

SourceSimulator.description: Class to generate simulated Source Estimates.

per_sample.description:
If True the metric is computed for each sample
separately. If False, the metric is spatio-temporal.

cosine_score.description: Compute cosine similarity between 2 source estimates.

threshold.description:
The threshold to apply to source estimates before computing
the dipole localization error. If a string the threshold is
a percentage and it should end with the percent character.

per_sample.description:
If True the metric is computed for each sample
separately. If False, the metric is spatio-temporal.

region_localization_error.description:
.. math::

    RLE = \frac{1}{2Q}\sum_{k \in I} \min_{l \in \hat{I}}{||r_k - r_l||} + \frac{1}{2\hat{Q}}\sum_{l \in \hat{I}} \min_{k \in I}{||r_k - r_l||}

where :math:`I` and :math:`\hat{I}` denote respectively the original and
estimated indexes of active sources, :math:`Q` and :math:`\hat{Q}` are
the numbers of original and estimated active sources.
:math:`r_k` denotes the position of the k-th source dipole in space
and :math:`||\cdot||` is an Euclidean norm in :math:`\mathbb{R}^3`.

threshold.description:
The threshold to apply to source estimates before computing
the f1 score. If a string the threshold is
a percentage and it should end with the percent character.

per_sample.description:
If True the metric is computed for each sample
separately. If False, the metric is spatio-temporal.

f1_score.description:
The F1 score can be interpreted as a weighted average of the precision
and recall, where an F1 score reaches its best value at 1 and worst score
at 0. The relative contribution of precision and recall to the F1
score are equal.
The formula for the F1 score is::

    F1 = 2 * (precision * recall) / (precision + recall)

Threshold is used first for data binarization.

threshold.description:
The threshold to apply to source estimates before computing
the precision. If a string the threshold is
a percentage and it should end with the percent character.

per_sample.description:
If True the metric is computed for each sample
separately. If False, the metric is spatio-temporal.

precision_score.description:
The precision is the ratio ``tp / (tp + fp)`` where ``tp`` is the number of
true positives and ``fp`` the number of false positives. The precision is
intuitively the ability of the classifier not to label as positive a sample
that is negative.

The best value is 1 and the worst value is 0.

Threshold is used first for data binarization.

threshold.description:
The threshold to apply to source estimates before computing
the recall. If a string the threshold is
a percentage and it should end with the percent character.

per_sample.description:
If True the metric is computed for each sample
separately. If False, the metric is spatio-temporal.

recall_score.description:
The recall is the ratio ``tp / (tp + fn)`` where ``tp`` is the number of
true positives and ``fn`` the number of false negatives. The recall is
intuitively the ability of the classifier to find all the positive samples.

The best value is 1 and the worst value is 0.

Threshold is used first for data binarization.

per_sample.description:
If True the metric is computed for each sample
separately. If False, the metric is spatio-temporal.

roc_auc_score.description:
ROC stands for receiver operating curve and AUC is Area under the curve.
When computing this metric the stc_true must be thresholded
as any non-zero value will be considered as a positive.

The ROC-AUC metric is computed between amplitudes of the source
estimates, i.e. after taking the absolute values.

threshold.description:
The threshold to apply to source estimates before computing
the recall. If a string the threshold is
a percentage and it should end with the percent character.

per_sample.description:
If True the metric is computed for each sample
separately. If False, the metric is spatio-temporal.

spatial_deviation_error.description:
The spatial deviation characterizes the spread of the estimate source
around the true source.

.. math::

    SD = \dfrac{\sum_i|s_i|\|r_{i} - r_{true}\|^2}{\sum_i|s_i|}.

where :math:`r_{true}` is a true dipole position,
:math:`r_i` and :math:`|s_i|` denote respectively the position
and amplitude of i-th dipole in source estimate.

Threshold is used on estimated source for focusing the metric to strong
amplitudes and omitting the low-amplitude values.

threshold.description:
The threshold to apply to source estimates before computing
the recall. If a string the threshold is
a percentage and it should end with the percent character.

per_sample.description:
If True the metric is computed for each sample
separately. If False, the metric is spatio-temporal.

peak_position_error.description:
The peak position error measures the distance between the center-of-mass
of the estimated and the true source.

.. math::

    PPE = \| \dfrac{\sum_i|s_i|r_{i}}{\sum_i|s_i|}
    - r_{true}\|,

where :math:`r_{true}` is a true dipole position,
:math:`r_i` and :math:`|s_i|` denote respectively the position
and amplitude of i-th dipole in source estimate.

Threshold is used on estimated source for focusing the metric to strong
amplitudes and omitting the low-amplitude values.

BiHemiLabel.description: A freesurfer/MNE label with vertices in both hemispheres.

hemi.description: Hemisphere to which the label applies.

comment.description: Kept as information but not used by the object itself.

name.description: Kept as information but not used by the object itself.

filename.description: Kept as information but not used by the object itself.

subject.description:
Subject which this label belongs to. Should only be specified if it is not
specified in the label.

verbose.description:
Control verbosity of the logging output. If ``None``, use the default
verbosity level. See the :ref:`logging documentation <tut-logging>` and
:func:`mne.verbose` for details. Should only be passed as a keyword
argument.

Label.description:
Labels can be combined with the ``+`` operator:

    * Duplicate vertices are removed.
    * If duplicate vertices have conflicting position values, an error
      is raised.
    * Values of duplicate vertices are summed.

subject.description:
The FreeSurfer subject name. While not necessary, it is safer to set the
subject parameter to avoid analysis errors.

verbose.description:
Control verbosity of the logging output. If ``None``, use the default
verbosity level. See the :ref:`logging documentation <tut-logging>` and
:func:`mne.verbose` for details. Should only be passed as a keyword
argument.

MixedSourceEstimate.description: Container for mixed surface and volume source estimates.

subject.description:
The FreeSurfer subject name. While not necessary, it is safer to set the
subject parameter to avoid analysis errors.

verbose.description:
Control verbosity of the logging output. If ``None``, use the default
verbosity level. See the :ref:`logging documentation <tut-logging>` and
:func:`mne.verbose` for details. Should only be passed as a keyword
argument.

subject.description:
The FreeSurfer subject name. While not necessary, it is safer to set the
subject parameter to avoid analysis errors.

verbose.description:
Control verbosity of the logging output. If ``None``, use the default
verbosity level. See the :ref:`logging documentation <tut-logging>` and
:func:`mne.verbose` for details. Should only be passed as a keyword
argument.

subject.description:
The FreeSurfer subject name. While not necessary, it is safer to set the
subject parameter to avoid analysis errors.

verbose.description:
Control verbosity of the logging output. If ``None``, use the default
verbosity level. See the :ref:`logging documentation <tut-logging>` and
:func:`mne.verbose` for details. Should only be passed as a keyword
argument.

VectorSourceEstimate.description:
For each vertex, the magnitude of the current is defined in the X, Y and Z
directions.

subject.description:
The FreeSurfer subject name. While not necessary, it is safer to set the
subject parameter to avoid analysis errors.

verbose.description:
Control verbosity of the logging output. If ``None``, use the default
verbosity level. See the :ref:`logging documentation <tut-logging>` and
:func:`mne.verbose` for details. Should only be passed as a keyword
argument.

subject.description:
The FreeSurfer subject name. While not necessary, it is safer to set the
subject parameter to avoid analysis errors.

verbose.description:
Control verbosity of the logging output. If ``None``, use the default
verbosity level. See the :ref:`logging documentation <tut-logging>` and
:func:`mne.verbose` for details. Should only be passed as a keyword
argument.

subject_from.description:
Name of the original subject as named in the SUBJECTS_DIR.
If None (default), then ``src[0]['subject_his_id]'`` will be used.

subject_to.description:
Name of the subject to which to morph as named in the SUBJECTS_DIR.
Default is ``'fsaverage'``. If None, ``src_to[0]['subject_his_id']``
will be used.

.. versionchanged:: 0.20
   Support for subject_to=None.

zooms.description:
The voxel size of volume for each spatial dimension in mm.
If spacing is None, MRIs won't be resliced, and both volumes
must have the same number of spatial dimensions.
Can also be ``'auto'`` to use ``5.`` if ``src_to is None`` and
the zooms from ``src_to`` otherwise.

.. versionchanged:: 0.20
   Support for 'auto' mode.

niter_affine.description:
Number of levels (``len(niter_affine)``) and number of
iterations per level - for each successive stage of iterative
refinement - to perform the affine transform.
Default is niter_affine=(100, 100, 10).

niter_sdr.description:
Number of levels (``len(niter_sdr)``) and number of
iterations per level - for each successive stage of iterative
refinement - to perform the Symmetric Diffeomorphic Registration (sdr)
transform. Default is niter_sdr=(5, 5, 3).

spacing.description:
The resolution of the icosahedral mesh (typically 5).
If None, all vertices will be used (potentially filling the
surface). If a list, then values will be morphed to the set of
vertices specified in in ``spacing[0]`` and ``spacing[1]``.
This will be ignored if ``src_to`` is supplied.

.. versionchanged:: 0.21
   src_to, if provided, takes precedence.

smooth.description:
Number of iterations for the smoothing of the surface data.
If None, smooth is automatically defined to fill the surface
with non-zero values. Can also be ``'nearest'`` to use the nearest
vertices on the surface.

.. versionchanged:: 0.20
   Added support for 'nearest'.

warn.description: If True, warn if not all vertices were used. The default is warn=True.

xhemi.description:
Morph across hemisphere. Currently only implemented for
``subject_to == subject_from``. See notes below.
The default is xhemi=False.

sparse.description:
Morph as a sparse source estimate. Works only with (Vector)
SourceEstimate. If True the only parameters used are subject_to and
subject_from, and spacing has to be None. Default is sparse=False.

precompute.description:
If True (default False), compute the sparse matrix representation of
the volumetric morph (if present). This takes a long time to
compute, but can make morphs faster when thousands of points are used.
See :meth:`mne.SourceMorph.compute_vol_morph_mat` (which can be called
later if desired) for more information.

.. versionadded:: 0.22

verbose.description:
Control verbosity of the logging output. If ``None``, use the default
verbosity level. See the :ref:`logging documentation <tut-logging>` and
:func:`mne.verbose` for details. Should only be passed as a keyword
argument.

compute_source_morph.description:
Method is based on spherical morphing by FreeSurfer for surface
cortical estimates :footcite:`GreveEtAl2013` and
Symmetric Diffeomorphic Registration for volumic data
:footcite:`AvantsEtAl2008`.

allow_empty.description:
``False`` (default) will emit an error if there are labels that have no
vertices in the source estimate. ``True`` and ``'ignore'`` will return
all-zero time courses for labels that do not have any vertices in the
source estimate, and True will emit a warning while and "ignore" will
just log a message.

.. versionchanged:: 0.21.0
   Support for "ignore".

return_generator.description: If True, a generator instead of a list is returned.

mri_resolution.description:
If True (default), the volume source space will be upsampled to the
original MRI resolution via trilinear interpolation before the atlas values
are extracted. This ensnures that each atlas label will contain source
activations. When False, only the original source space points are used,
and some atlas labels thus may not contain any source space vertices.

.. versionadded:: 0.21.0

verbose.description:
Control verbosity of the logging output. If ``None``, use the default
verbosity level. See the :ref:`logging documentation <tut-logging>` and
:func:`mne.verbose` for details. Should only be passed as a keyword
argument.

extract_label_time_course.description:
This function will extract one time course for each label and source
estimate. The way the time courses are extracted depends on the mode
parameter (see Notes).

verbose.description:
Control verbosity of the logging output. If ``None``, use the default
verbosity level. See the :ref:`logging documentation <tut-logging>` and
:func:`mne.verbose` for details. Should only be passed as a keyword
argument.

grade_to_tris.description: Get tris defined for a certain grade.

n_jobs.description:
The number of jobs to run in parallel. If ``-1``, it is set
to the number of CPU cores. Requires the :mod:`joblib` package.
``None`` (default) is a marker for 'unset' that will be interpreted
as ``n_jobs=1`` (sequential execution) unless the call is performed under
a :class:`joblib:joblib.parallel_config` context manager that sets another
value for ``n_jobs``.

verbose.description:
Control verbosity of the logging output. If ``None``, use the default
verbosity level. See the :ref:`logging documentation <tut-logging>` and
:func:`mne.verbose` for details. Should only be passed as a keyword
argument.

grade_to_vertices.description: Convert a grade to source space vertices for a given subject.

location.description:
Location to grow label from. If the location is an int, it represents
the vertex number in the corresponding label. If it is a str, it can be
either 'random' or 'center'.

extent.description:
Extents (radius in mm) of the labels, i.e. maximum geodesic distance
on the white matter surface from the seed. If 0, the resulting label
will contain only one vertex.

grow_outside.description:
Let the region grow outside the original label where location was
defined.

name.description: Assign name to the new label.

surf.description: The surface used to simulated the label, defaults to the white surface.

n_jobs.description:
The number of jobs to run in parallel. If ``-1``, it is set
to the number of CPU cores. Requires the :mod:`joblib` package.
``None`` (default) is a marker for 'unset' that will be interpreted
as ``n_jobs=1`` (sequential execution) unless the call is performed under
a :class:`joblib:joblib.parallel_config` context manager that sets another
value for ``n_jobs``.
Likely only useful if tens or hundreds of labels are being expanded
simultaneously. Does not apply with ``overlap=False``.

overlap.description:
Produce overlapping labels. If True (default), the resulting labels
can be overlapping. If False, each label will be grown one step at a
time, and occupied territory will not be invaded.

names.description:
Assign names to the new labels (list needs to have the same length as
seeds).

surface.description:
The surface along which to do the computations, defaults to ``'white'``
(the gray-white matter boundary).

grow_labels.description:
This function generates a number of labels in source space by growing
regions starting from the vertices defined in "seeds". For each seed, a
label is generated containing all vertices within a maximum geodesic
distance on the white matter surface from the seed.

tmin.description: The tmin to use for the STC.

tstep.description: The tstep to use for the STC.

subject.description: The FreeSurfer subject name.

verbose.description:
Control verbosity of the logging output. If ``None``, use the default
verbosity level. See the :ref:`logging documentation <tut-logging>` and
:func:`mne.verbose` for details. Should only be passed as a keyword
argument.

labels_to_stc.description:
This function is meant to work like the opposite of
`extract_label_time_course`.

subject_from.description:
The subject to morph labels from. Can be None if the labels
have the ``.subject`` property defined.

surf_name.description: Surface used to obtain vertex locations, e.g., ``'white'``, ``'pial'``.

verbose.description:
Control verbosity of the logging output. If ``None``, use the default
verbosity level. See the :ref:`logging documentation <tut-logging>` and
:func:`mne.verbose` for details. Should only be passed as a keyword
argument.

morph_labels.description:
This is useful when morphing a set of non-overlapping labels (such as those
obtained with :func:`read_labels_from_annot`) from one subject to
another.

surface.description:
The surface along which to do the computations, defaults to ``'white'``
(the gray-white matter boundary).

random_parcellation.description:
This function generates a number of labels which don't intersect and
cover the whole surface. Regions are growing around randomly chosen
seeds.

read_source_morph.description: Load the morph for source estimates from a file.

subject.description:
Subject which this label belongs to. Should only be specified if it is not
specified in the label.

freesurfer.description:
By default (``False``) ``split_label`` uses an algorithm that is
slightly optimized for performance and numerical precision. Set
``freesurfer`` to ``True`` in order to replicate label splits from
FreeSurfer's ``mris_divide_parcellation``.

split_label.description: Split a Label into two or more parts.

smooth.description:
Fill in vertices on the cortical surface that are not in the source
space based on the closest source space vertex (requires
src to be a SourceSpace).

connected.description:
If True a list of connected labels will be returned in each
hemisphere. The labels are ordered in decreasing order depending
of the maximum value in the stc.

verbose.description:
Control verbosity of the logging output. If ``None``, use the default
verbosity level. See the :ref:`logging documentation <tut-logging>` and
:func:`mne.verbose` for details. Should only be passed as a keyword
argument.

stc_to_label.description: Compute a label from the non-zero sources in an stc object.

distance.description: Distance (m) defining the activation "ball" of the sensor.

mode.description:
Can be ``"sum"`` to do a linear sum of weights, ``"weighted"`` to make
this a weighted sum, ``"nearest"`` to use only the weight of the
nearest sensor, or ``"single"`` to do a distance-weight of the nearest
sensor. Default is ``"sum"``. See Notes.

.. versionchanged:: 0.24
   Added "weighted" option.

project.description:
If True, project the sensors to the nearest ``'pial`` surface
vertex before computing distances. Only used when doing a
surface projection.

surface.description:
The surface to use. If ``src=None``, defaults to the pial surface.
Otherwise, the source space surface will be used.

.. versionadded:: 0.24.1

verbose.description:
Control verbosity of the logging output. If ``None``, use the default
verbosity level. See the :ref:`logging documentation <tut-logging>` and
:func:`mne.verbose` for details. Should only be passed as a keyword
argument.

stc_near_sensors.description: Create a STC from ECoG, sEEG and DBS sensor data.

copy.description: If False (default), operate in-place.

transform_surface_to.description: Transform surface to the desired coordinate system.

verbose.description:
Control verbosity of the logging output. If ``None``, use the default
verbosity level. See the :ref:`logging documentation <tut-logging>` and
:func:`mne.verbose` for details. Should only be passed as a keyword
argument.

compute_distance_to_sensors.description: Compute distances between vertices and sensors.

get_decimated_surfaces.description: Get the decimated surfaces from a source space.

sigma.description:
The variance estimate will be given by ``var + sigma * max(var)`` or
``var + sigma``, depending on "method". By default this is 0 (no
adjustment). See Notes for details.

method.description:
If 'relative', the minimum variance estimate will be sigma * max(var),
if 'absolute' the minimum variance estimate will be sigma.

ttest_1samp_no_p.description:
This is a modified version of :func:`scipy.stats.ttest_1samp` that avoids
a (relatively) time-consuming p-value calculation, and can adjust
for implausibly small variance values :footcite:`RidgwayEtAl2012`.

ttest_ind_no_p.description:
This is a modified version of :func:`scipy.stats.ttest_ind`. It operates
along the first axis. The ``sigma`` parameter provides an optional "hat"
adjustment (see :func:`ttest_1samp_no_p` and :footcite:`RidgwayEtAl2012`).

sigma.description:
Regularization parameter (>= 0) added to the within-group mean
square to mitigate F-statistic inflation under low-variance
conditions. ``0`` (default) disables correction.

.. versionadded:: 1.12

method.description:
How *sigma* is interpreted when ``sigma > 0``. Can be
``'relative'`` (default) or ``'absolute'``.
``'relative'`` multiplies *sigma* by the median within-group
mean square (scale-invariant, recommended).
``'absolute'`` uses *sigma* directly as a raw sigma squared.

.. versionadded:: 1.12

f_oneway.description:
The one-way ANOVA tests the null hypothesis that 2 or more groups have
the same population mean. The test is applied to samples from two or
more groups, possibly with differing sizes :footcite:`Lowry2014`.

This is a modified version of :func:`scipy.stats.f_oneway` that avoids
computing the associated p-value.

effects.description:
A string denoting the effect to be returned. The following
mapping is currently supported (example with 2 factors):

    * ``'A'``: main effect of A
    * ``'B'``: main effect of B
    * ``'A:B'``: interaction effect
    * ``'A+B'``: both main effects
    * ``'A*B'``: all three effects
    * ``'all'``: all effects (equals 'A*B' in a 2 way design)

If list, effect names are used: ``['A', 'B', 'A:B']``.

correction.description:
The correction method to be employed if one factor has more than two
levels. If True, sphericity correction using the Greenhouse-Geisser
method will be applied.

return_pvals.description: If True, return p-values corresponding to F-values.

f_mway_rm.description: Compute M-way repeated measures ANOVA for fully balanced designs.

effects.description:
A string denoting the effect to be returned. The following
mapping is currently supported:

    * ``'A'``: main effect of A
    * ``'B'``: main effect of B
    * ``'A:B'``: interaction effect
    * ``'A+B'``: both main effects
    * ``'A*B'``: all three effects

f_threshold_mway_rm.description: Compute F-value thresholds for a two-way ANOVA.

event_id.description:
As in Epochs; a dictionary where the values may be integers or
iterables of integers, corresponding to the 3rd column of
events, and the keys are condition names.
If None, uses all events in the events array.

tmin.description:
If float, gives the lower limit (in seconds) for the time window for
which all event types' effects are estimated. If a dict, can be used to
specify time windows for specific event types: keys correspond to keys
in event_id and/or covariates; for missing values, the default (-.1) is
used.

tmax.description:
If float, gives the upper limit (in seconds) for the time window for
which all event types' effects are estimated. If a dict, can be used to
specify time windows for specific event types: keys correspond to keys
in event_id and/or covariates; for missing values, the default (1.) is
used.

reject.description:
For cleaning raw data before the regression is performed: set up
rejection parameters based on peak-to-peak amplitude in continuously
selected subepochs. If None, no rejection is done.
If dict, keys are types ('grad' | 'mag' | 'eeg' | 'eog' | 'ecg')
and values are the maximal peak-to-peak values to select rejected
epochs, e.g.::

    reject = dict(grad=4000e-12, # T / m (gradiometers)
                  mag=4e-11, # T (magnetometers)
                  eeg=40e-5, # V (EEG channels)
                  eog=250e-5 # V (EOG channels))

flat.description:
For cleaning raw data before the regression is performed: set up
rejection parameters based on flatness of the signal. If None, no
rejection is done. If a dict, keys are ('grad' | 'mag' |
'eeg' | 'eog' | 'ecg') and values are minimal peak-to-peak values to
select rejected epochs.

tstep.description: Length of windows for peak-to-peak detection for raw data cleaning.

decim.description:
Decimate by choosing only a subsample of data points. Highly
recommended for data recorded at high sampling frequencies, as
otherwise huge intermediate matrices have to be created and inverted.

linear_regression_raw.description:
This models the full M/EEG time course, including correction for
overlapping potentials and allowing for continuous/scalar predictors.
Internally, this constructs a predictor matrix X of size
n_samples * (n_conds * window length), solving the linear system
``Y = bX`` and returning ``b`` as evoked-like time series split by
condition. See :footcite:`SmithKutas2015`.

method.description:
If 'indep' it implements Benjamini/Hochberg for independent or if
'negcorr' it corresponds to Benjamini/Yekutieli.

fdr_correction.description:
Correction for multiple comparison using FDR :footcite:`GenoveseEtAl2002`.

This covers Benjamini/Hochberg for independent or positively correlated and
Benjamini/Yekutieli for general or negatively correlated tests.

combine_adjacency.description: Create a sparse binary adjacency/neighbors matrix.

threshold.description:
The so-called "cluster forming threshold" in the form of a test statistic
(note: this is not an alpha level / "p-value").
If numeric, vertices with data values more extreme than ``threshold`` will
be used to form clusters. If ``None``, an F-threshold will be chosen
automatically that corresponds to a p-value of 0.05 for the given number of
observations (only valid when using an F-statistic). If ``threshold`` is a
:class:`dict` (with keys ``'start'`` and ``'step'``) then threshold-free
cluster enhancement (TFCE) will be used (see the
:ref:`TFCE example <tfce_example>` and :footcite:`SmithNichols2009`).
See Notes for an example on how to compute a threshold based on
a particular p-value for one-tailed or two-tailed tests.

n_permutations.description: The number of permutations to compute.

tail.description:
If tail is 1, the statistic is thresholded above threshold.
If tail is -1, the statistic is thresholded below threshold.
If tail is 0, the statistic is thresholded on both sides of
the distribution.

n_jobs.description:
The number of jobs to run in parallel. If ``-1``, it is set
to the number of CPU cores. Requires the :mod:`joblib` package.
``None`` (default) is a marker for 'unset' that will be interpreted
as ``n_jobs=1`` (sequential execution) unless the call is performed under
a :class:`joblib:joblib.parallel_config` context manager that sets another
value for ``n_jobs``.

max_step.description:
Maximum distance between samples along the second axis of ``X`` to be
considered adjacent (typically the second axis is the "time" dimension).
Only used when ``adjacency`` has shape (n_vertices, n_vertices), that is,
when adjacency is only specified for sensors (e.g., via
:func:`mne.channels.find_ch_adjacency`), and not via sensors **and**
further dimensions such as time points (e.g., via an additional call of
:func:`mne.stats.combine_adjacency`).

step_down_p.description:
To perform a step-down-in-jumps test, pass a p-value for clusters to
exclude from each successive iteration. Default is zero, perform no
step-down test (since no clusters will be smaller than this value).
Setting this to a reasonable value, e.g. 0.05, can increase sensitivity
but costs computation time.

t_power.description:
Power to raise the statistical values (usually F-values) by before
summing (sign will be retained). Note that ``t_power=0`` will give a
count of locations in each cluster, ``t_power=1`` will weight each location
by its statistical score.

out_type.description:
Output format of clusters within a list.
If ``'mask'``, returns a list of boolean arrays,
each with the same shape as the input data (or slices if the shape is 1D
and adjacency is None), with ``True`` values indicating locations that are
part of a cluster. If ``'indices'``, returns a list of tuple of ndarray,
where each ndarray contains the indices of locations that together form the
given cluster along the given dimension. Note that for large datasets,
``'indices'`` may use far less memory than ``'mask'``.
Default is ``'indices'``.

check_disjoint.description:
Whether to check if the connectivity matrix can be separated into disjoint
sets before clustering. This may lead to faster clustering, especially if
the second dimension of ``X`` (usually the "time" dimension) is large.

buffer_size.description:
Block size to use when computing test statistics. This can significantly
reduce memory usage when ``n_jobs > 1`` and memory sharing between
processes is enabled (see :func:`mne.set_cache_dir`), because ``X`` will be
shared between processes and each process only needs to allocate space for
a small block of locations at a time.

verbose.description:
Control verbosity of the logging output. If ``None``, use the default
verbosity level. See the :ref:`logging documentation <tut-logging>` and
:func:`mne.verbose` for details. Should only be passed as a keyword
argument.

permutation_cluster_test.description:
For a list of :class:`NumPy arrays <numpy.ndarray>` of data,
calculate some statistics corrected for multiple comparisons using
permutations and cluster-level correction. Each element of the list ``X``
should contain the data for one group of observations (e.g., 2D arrays for
time series, 3D arrays for time-frequency power values). Permutations are
generated with random partitions of the data. For details, see
:footcite:p:`MarisOostenveld2007,Sassenhagen2019`.

threshold.description:
The so-called "cluster forming threshold" in the form of a test statistic
(note: this is not an alpha level / "p-value").
If numeric, vertices with data values more extreme than ``threshold`` will
be used to form clusters. If ``None``, a t-threshold will be chosen
automatically that corresponds to a p-value of 0.05 for the given number of
observations (only valid when using a t-statistic). If ``threshold`` is a
:class:`dict` (with keys ``'start'`` and ``'step'``) then threshold-free
cluster enhancement (TFCE) will be used (see the
:ref:`TFCE example <tfce_example>` and :footcite:`SmithNichols2009`).
See Notes for an example on how to compute a threshold based on
a particular p-value for one-tailed or two-tailed tests.

n_permutations.description:
The number of permutations to compute. Can be 'all' to perform
an exact test.

tail.description:
If tail is 1, the statistic is thresholded above threshold.
If tail is -1, the statistic is thresholded below threshold.
If tail is 0, the statistic is thresholded on both sides of
the distribution.

n_jobs.description:
The number of jobs to run in parallel. If ``-1``, it is set
to the number of CPU cores. Requires the :mod:`joblib` package.
``None`` (default) is a marker for 'unset' that will be interpreted
as ``n_jobs=1`` (sequential execution) unless the call is performed under

value for ``n_jobs``.

further dimensions such as time points (e.g., via an additional call of
:func:`mne.stats.combine_adjacency`).

verbose.description:
Control verbosity of the logging output. If ``None``, use the default
verbosity level. See the :ref:`logging documentation <tut-logging>` and
:func:`mne.verbose` for details. Should only be passed as a keyword
argument.

n_permutations.description:
Number of permutations. If n_permutations is 'all' all possible
permutations are tested. It's the exact test, that
can be untractable when the number of samples is big (e.g. > 20).
If n_permutations >= 2**n_samples then the exact test is performed.

n_jobs.description:
The number of jobs to run in parallel. If ``-1``, it is set
to the number of CPU cores. Requires the :mod:`joblib` package.
``None`` (default) is a marker for 'unset' that will be interpreted
as ``n_jobs=1`` (sequential execution) unless the call is performed under
a :class:`joblib:joblib.parallel_config` context manager that sets another
value for ``n_jobs``.

verbose.description:

:func:`mne.verbose` for details. Should only be passed as a keyword
argument.

permutation_t_test.description:
This function can perform the test on one variable or
simultaneously on multiple variables. When applying the test to multiple
variables, the "tmax" method is used for adjusting the p-values of each
variable for multiple comparisons. Like Bonferroni correction, this method
adjusts p-values in a way that controls the family-wise error rate.
However, the permutation method will be more
powerful than Bonferroni correction when different variables in the test
are correlated (see :footcite:`NicholsHolmes2002`).

threshold.description:
The so-called "cluster forming threshold" in the form of a test statistic

automatically that corresponds to a p-value of 0.05 for the given number of

cluster enhancement (TFCE) will be used (see the
:ref:`TFCE example <tfce_example>` and :footcite:`SmithNichols2009`).
See Notes for an example on how to compute a threshold based on
a particular p-value for one-tailed or two-tailed tests.

spatio_temporal_cluster_test.description:
This function provides a convenient wrapper for
:func:`mne.stats.permutation_cluster_test`, for use with data
organized in the form (observations × time × space),
(observations × time × space), or optionally
(observations × time × frequencies × space). For more information,
see :footcite:p:`MarisOostenveld2007,Sassenhagen2019`.

threshold.description:
The so-called "cluster forming threshold" in the form of a test statistic
(note: this is not an alpha level / "p-value").
If numeric, vertices with data values more extreme than ``threshold`` will

observations (only valid when using a t-statistic). If ``threshold`` is a

cluster enhancement (TFCE) will be used (see the
:ref:`TFCE example <tfce_example>` and :footcite:`SmithNichols2009`).
See Notes for an example on how to compute a threshold based on
a particular p-value for one-tailed or two-tailed tests.

The number of permutations to compute. Can be 'all' to perform
an exact test.

tail.description:

If tail is -1, the statistic is thresholded below threshold.
If tail is 0, the statistic is thresholded on both sides of
the distribution.

This function provides a convenient wrapper for
:func:`mne.stats.permutation_cluster_1samp_test`, for use with data
organized in the form (observations × time × space),
(observations × frequencies × space), or optionally
(observations × time × frequencies × space). For details, see
:footcite:p:`MarisOostenveld2007,Sassenhagen2019`.

p_thresh.description: The significance threshold for inclusion of clusters.

tstep.description:
The time step between samples of the original :class:`STC
<mne.SourceEstimate>`, in seconds (i.e., ``1 / stc.sfreq``). Defaults
to ``1``, which will yield a colormap indicating cluster duration
measured in *samples* rather than *seconds*.

tmin.description: The time of the first sample.

subject.description: The name of the subject.

summarize_clusters_stc.description:
This helps visualizing results from spatio-temporal-clustering
permutation tests.

ci.description: Level of the confidence interval between 0 and 1.

bootstrap_confidence_interval.description: Get confidence intervals from non-parametric bootstrap.

start.description:
Start time (in s) of the time window used for SME computation. If ``None``, use
the start of the epoch.

stop.description:
Stop time (in s) of the time window used for SME computation. If ``None``, use
the end of the epoch.

compute_sme.description:
The standardized measurement error :footcite:`LuckEtAl2021` can be used as a
universal measure of data quality in ERP studies.

verbose.description:
Control verbosity of the logging output. If ``None``, use the default
verbosity level. See the :ref:`logging documentation <tut-logging>` and
:func:`mne.verbose` for details. Should only be passed as a keyword
argument.

spatial_dist_adjacency.description: Compute adjacency from distances in a source space.

verbose.description:

to process a subset of triangles. Defaults to False.

tmin.description:
First and last times to include, in seconds. ``None`` uses the first or
last time present in the data. Default is ``tmin=None, tmax=None`` (all
times).

tmax.description:
First and last times to include, in seconds. ``None`` uses the first or
last time present in the data. Default is ``tmin=None, tmax=None`` (all
times).

proj.description:
Whether to apply SSP projection vectors before spectral estimation.
Default is ``False``.

comment.description:
Comment on the data, e.g., the experimental condition(s)averaged.Default is ``None``
which is replaced with ``inst.comment`` (for :class:`~mne.Evoked` instances)
or a comma-separated string representation of the keys in ``inst.event_id``
(for :class:`~mne.Epochs` instances).

n_jobs.description:
The number of jobs to run in parallel. If ``-1``, it is set
to the number of CPU cores. Requires the :mod:`joblib` package.
``None`` (default) is a marker for 'unset' that will be interpreted
as ``n_jobs=1`` (sequential execution) unless the call is performed under

argument.

AverageTFR.description:
.. warning:: The preferred means of creating AverageTFR objects is via the
             instance methods :meth:`mne.Epochs.compute_tfr` and
             :meth:`mne.Evoked.compute_tfr`, or via
             :meth:`mne.time_frequency.EpochsTFR.average`. Direct class
             instantiation is discouraged.

comment.description: Comment on the data, e.g., the experimental condition(s) averaged.

method.description:
Comment on the method used to compute the data, e.g., ``"hilbert"``.
Default is ``None``.

AverageTFRArray.description: Data object for *precomputed* spectrotemporal representations of averaged data.

tmin.description:
First and last times to include, in seconds. ``None`` uses the first or
last time present in the data. Default is ``tmin=None, tmax=None`` (all
times).

tmax.description:
First and last times to include, in seconds. ``None`` uses the first or
last time present in the data. Default is ``tmin=None, tmax=None`` (all
times).

proj.description:
Whether to apply SSP projection vectors before spectral estimation.
Default is ``False``.

n_jobs.description:
The number of jobs to run in parallel. If ``-1``, it is set
to the number of CPU cores. Requires the :mod:`joblib` package.
``None`` (default) is a marker for 'unset' that will be interpreted

verbose.description:

EpochsTFR.description:
.. important::
    The preferred means of creating EpochsTFR objects from :class:`~mne.Epochs`
    objects is via the instance method :meth:`~mne.Epochs.compute_tfr`.
    To create an EpochsTFR object from pre-computed data (i.e., a NumPy array) use
    :class:`~mne.time_frequency.EpochsTFRArray`.

comment.description: Comment on the data, e.g., the experimental condition(s).

method.description:
Comment on the method used to compute the data, e.g., ``"hilbert"``.
Default is ``None``.

event_id.description:
Mapping from condition descriptions (strings) to integer event codes.If ``None``,
all events in ``events`` will be included, and the ``event_id`` attribute
will be a :class:`dict` mapping a string version of each integer event ID
to the corresponding integer.

drop_log.description:
Tuple of tuple of strings indicating which epochs have been marked to
be ignored.

EpochsTFRArray.description: Data object for *precomputed* spectrotemporal representations of epoched data.

tmin.description:
First and last times to include, in seconds. ``None`` uses the first or
last time present in the data. Default is ``tmin=None, tmax=None`` (all
times).

tmax.description:
First and last times to include, in seconds. ``None`` uses the first or
last time present in the data. Default is ``tmin=None, tmax=None`` (all
times).

proj.description:
Whether to apply SSP projection vectors before spectral estimation.
Default is ``False``.

reject_by_annotation.description:
Whether to omit bad spans of data before spectrotemporal power
estimation. If ``True``, spans with annotations whose description
begins with ``bad`` will be represented with ``np.nan`` in the
time-frequency representation.

n_jobs.description:
The number of jobs to run in parallel. If ``-1``, it is set
to the number of CPU cores. Requires the :mod:`joblib` package.
``None`` (default) is a marker for 'unset' that will be interpreted
as ``n_jobs=1`` (sequential execution) unless the call is performed under
a :class:`joblib:joblib.parallel_config` context manager that sets another
value for ``n_jobs``.

:func:`mne.verbose` for details. Should only be passed as a keyword
argument.

RawTFR.description:
.. warning:: The preferred means of creating RawTFR objects from
             :class:`~mne.io.Raw` objects is via the instance method
             :meth:`~mne.io.Raw.compute_tfr`. Direct class instantiation
             is not supported.

method.description:
Comment on the method used to compute the data, e.g., ``"hilbert"``.
Default is ``None``.

RawTFRArray.description: Data object for *precomputed* spectrotemporal representations of continuous data.

tmin.description:
Start of the time window for which CSD was calculated in seconds. Can
be ``None`` (the default) to indicate no timing information is
available.

tmax.description:
End of the time window for which CSD was calculated in seconds. Can be
``None`` (the default) to indicate no timing information is available.

CrossSpectralDensity.description:
Given a list of time series, the CSD matrix denotes for each pair of time
series, the cross-spectral density. This matrix is symmetric and internally
stored as a vector.

This object can store multiple CSD matrices: one for each frequency.
Use ``.get_data(freq)`` to obtain an CSD matrix as an ndarray.

verbose.description:
Control verbosity of the logging output. If ``None``, use the default
verbosity level. See the :ref:`logging documentation <tut-logging>` and
:func:`mne.verbose` for details. Should only be passed as a keyword
argument.

Spectrum.description:
.. warning:: The preferred means of creating Spectrum objects from
             continuous or averaged data is via the instance methods
             :meth:`mne.io.Raw.compute_psd` or
             :meth:`mne.Evoked.compute_psd`. Direct class instantiation
             is not supported.

dim_names.description:
The name of the dimensions in the data, in the order they occur. Must contain
``'channel'`` and ``'freq'``;  if data are unaggregated estimates, also include
either a ``'segment'`` (e.g., Welch-like algorithms) or ``'taper'`` (e.g.,
multitaper algorithms) dimension. If including ``'taper'``, you should also pass
a ``weights`` parameter.

.. versionadded:: 1.8

verbose.description:
Control verbosity of the logging output. If ``None``, use the default
verbosity level. See the :ref:`logging documentation <tut-logging>` and
:func:`mne.verbose` for details. Should only be passed as a keyword
argument.

SpectrumArray.description: Data object for precomputed spectral data (in NumPy array format).

event_id.description:
The id of the :term:`events` to consider. If dict, the keys can later be
used to access associated :term:`events`. Example:
dict(auditory=1, visual=3). If int, a dict will be created with the id as
string. If a list of int, all :term:`events` with the IDs specified in the list
are used. If a str or list of str, ``events`` must be ``None`` to use annotations
and then the IDs must be the name(s) of the annotations to use.
If None, all :term:`events` will be used and a dict is created
with string integer names corresponding to the event id integers.

dim_names.description:
The name of the dimensions in the data, in the order they occur. Must contain
``'channel'`` and ``'freq'``;  if data are unaggregated estimates, also include
either a ``'segment'`` (e.g., Welch-like algorithms) or ``'taper'`` (e.g.,
multitaper algorithms) dimension. If including ``'taper'``, you should also pass
a ``weights`` parameter.

.. versionadded:: 1.8

verbose.description:
Control verbosity of the logging output. If ``None``, use the default
verbosity level. See the :ref:`logging documentation <tut-logging>` and
:func:`mne.verbose` for details. Should only be passed as a keyword
argument.

EpochsSpectrumArray.description: Data object for precomputed epoched spectral data (in NumPy array format).

weights.description:
The weights to apply to the data of each :class:`~mne.time_frequency.Spectrum`
instance, or a string describing the weighting strategy to apply: 'nave'
computes sum-to-one weights proportional to each object’s nave attribute;
'equal' weights each :class:`~mne.time_frequency.Spectrum` by
``1 / len(all_spectrum)``.

combine_spectrum.description:
Create a new :class:`mne.time_frequency.Spectrum` instance, using a combination of
the supplied instances as its data. By default, the mean (weighted by trials) is
used. Subtraction can be performed by passing negative weights (e.g., ``[1, -1]``).
Data must have the same channels and the same frequencies.

weights.description:
The weights to apply to the data of each AverageTFR instance.
Can also be ``'nave'`` to weight according to tfr.nave,
or ``'equal'`` to use equal weighting (each weighted as ``1/N``).

combine_tfr.description:
Create a new :class:`mne.time_frequency.AverageTFR` instance, using a combination of
the supplied instances as its data. By default, the mean (weighted by trials) is
used. Subtraction can be performed by passing negative weights (e.g., [1, -1]). Data
must have the same channels and the same time instants.

tmin.description:
Minimum time instant to consider, in seconds. If ``None`` start at
first sample.

tmax.description:
Maximum time instant to consider, in seconds. If ``None`` end at last
sample.

verbose.description:
Control verbosity of the logging output. If ``None``, use the default

n_jobs.description:
The number of jobs to run in parallel. If ``-1``, it is set
to the number of CPU cores. Requires the :mod:`joblib` package.
``None`` (default) is a marker for 'unset' that will be interpreted
as ``n_jobs=1`` (sequential execution) unless the call is performed under
a :class:`joblib:joblib.parallel_config` context manager that sets another
value for ``n_jobs``.

first sample.

Maximum time instant to consider, in seconds. If ``None`` end at last
sample.

n_fft.description:
Length of the FFT. If ``None``, the exact number of samples between
``tmin`` and ``tmax`` will be used.

Minimum time instant to consider, in seconds. If ``None`` start at
first sample.

tmax.description:
Maximum time instant to consider, in seconds. If ``None`` end at last
sample.

Number of cycles to use when constructing Morlet wavelets. Fixed number
or one per frequency. Defaults to 7.

use_fft.description:
Whether to use FFT-based convolution to compute the wavelet transform.
Defaults to True.

n_jobs.description:
The number of jobs to run in parallel. If ``-1``, it is set
to the number of CPU cores. Requires the :mod:`joblib` package.

csd_morlet.description: Estimate cross-spectral density from epochs using Morlet wavelets.

include.description: List of channels to include (if empty, include all available).

exclude.description: Channels to exclude (if empty, do not exclude any).

ordered.description:
If True (default), ensure that the order of the channels in
the modified instance matches the order of ``ch_names``.

.. versionadded:: 0.20.0
.. versionchanged:: 1.7
    The default changed from False in 1.6 to True in 1.7.

copy.description:
If True (the default), return a copy of the CSD matrix with the
modified channels. If False, channels are modified in-place.

.. versionadded:: 0.20.0

verbose.description:
Control verbosity of the logging output. If ``None``, use the default
verbosity level. See the :ref:`logging documentation <tut-logging>` and
:func:`mne.verbose` for details. Should only be passed as a keyword
argument.

pick_channels_csd.description: Pick channels from cross-spectral density matrix.

read_csd.description: Read a CrossSpectralDensity object from an HDF5 file.

tmin.description: The beginning of time interval in seconds.

tmax.description: The end of time interval in seconds.

verbose.description:
Control verbosity of the logging output. If ``None``, use the default
verbosity level. See the :ref:`logging documentation <tut-logging>` and
:func:`mne.verbose` for details. Should only be passed as a keyword
argument.

fit_iir_model_raw.description:
The computed filter is fitted to data from all of the picked channels,
with frequency response given by the standard IIR formula:

.. math::

    H(e^{jw}) = \frac{1}{a[0] + a[1]e^{-jw} + ... + a[n]e^{-jnw}}

use_fft.description: The fft based convolution or not.

return_itc.description:
Return inter-trial coherence (ITC) as well as averaged power.
Must be ``False`` for evoked data.

n_jobs.description:
The number of jobs to run in parallel. If ``-1``, it is set
to the number of CPU cores. Requires the :mod:`joblib` package.
``None`` (default) is a marker for 'unset' that will be interpreted
as ``n_jobs=1`` (sequential execution) unless the call is performed under
a :class:`joblib:joblib.parallel_config` context manager that sets another
value for ``n_jobs``.

zero_mean.description:
Make sure the wavelet has a mean of zero.

.. versionadded:: 0.13.0

average.description:
If ``False`` return an `EpochsTFR` containing separate TFRs for each
epoch. If ``True`` return an `AverageTFR` containing the average of all
TFRs across epochs.

.. note::
    Using ``average=True`` is functionally equivalent to using
    ``average=False`` followed by ``EpochsTFR.average()``, but is
    more memory efficient.

.. versionadded:: 0.13.0

output.description:
Can be ``"power"`` (default) or ``"complex"``. If ``"complex"``, then
``average`` must be ``False``.

.. versionadded:: 0.15.0

verbose.description:
Control verbosity of the logging output. If ``None``, use the default
verbosity level. See the :ref:`logging documentation <tut-logging>` and
:func:`mne.verbose` for details. Should only be passed as a keyword
argument.

tfr_morlet.description:
Compute Time-Frequency Representation (TFR) using Morlet wavelets.

Same computation as `~mne.time_frequency.tfr_array_morlet`, but
operates on `~mne.Epochs` or `~mne.Evoked` objects instead of
:class:`NumPy arrays <numpy.ndarray>`.

use_fft.description: The fft based convolution or not.

return_itc.description:
Return inter-trial coherence (ITC) as well as averaged (or
single-trial) power.

n_jobs.description:
The number of jobs to run in parallel. If ``-1``, it is set
to the number of CPU cores. Requires the :mod:`joblib` package.
``None`` (default) is a marker for 'unset' that will be interpreted
as ``n_jobs=1`` (sequential execution) unless the call is performed under
a :class:`joblib:joblib.parallel_config` context manager that sets another
value for ``n_jobs``.

average.description:
If ``False`` return an `EpochsTFR` containing separate TFRs for each
epoch. If ``True`` return an `AverageTFR` containing the average of all
TFRs across epochs.

.. note::
    Using ``average=True`` is functionally equivalent to using
    ``average=False`` followed by ``EpochsTFR.average()``, but is
    more memory efficient.

.. versionadded:: 0.13.0

verbose.description:
Control verbosity of the logging output. If ``None``, use the default
verbosity level. See the :ref:`logging documentation <tut-logging>` and
:func:`mne.verbose` for details. Should only be passed as a keyword
argument.

tfr_multitaper.description:
Compute Time-Frequency Representation (TFR) using DPSS tapers.

Same computation as :func:`~mne.time_frequency.tfr_array_multitaper`, but
operates on :class:`~mne.Epochs` or :class:`~mne.Evoked` objects instead of
:class:`NumPy arrays <numpy.ndarray>`.

fmin.description:
The minimum frequency to include. If None defaults to the minimum fft
frequency greater than zero.

fmax.description: The maximum frequency to include. If None defaults to the maximum fft.

n_fft.description:
The length of the windows used for FFT. If None, it defaults to the
next power of 2 larger than the signal length.

width.description:
The width of the Gaussian window. If < 1, increased temporal
resolution, if > 1, increased frequency resolution. Defaults to 1.
(classical S-Transform).

decim.description: The decimation factor on the time axis. To reduce memory usage.

return_itc.description: Return intertrial coherence (ITC) as well as averaged power.

n_jobs.description: The number of jobs to run in parallel (over channels).

verbose.description:
Control verbosity of the logging output. If ``None``, use the default
verbosity level. See the :ref:`logging documentation <tut-logging>` and
:func:`mne.verbose` for details. Should only be passed as a keyword
argument.

tfr_stockwell.description:
Compute Time-Frequency Representation (TFR) using Stockwell Transform.

Same computation as `~mne.time_frequency.tfr_array_stockwell`, but operates
on `~mne.Epochs` objects instead of :class:`NumPy arrays <numpy.ndarray>`.

See :footcite:`Stockwell2007,MoukademEtAl2014,WheatEtAl2010,JonesEtAl2006`
for more information.

condition.description:
The condition to load. If ``None``, all conditions will be returned.
Defaults to ``None``.

verbose.description:

overwrite.description:

Time of the first sample relative to the onset of the epoch, in
seconds. Defaults to 0.

Minimum time instant to consider, in seconds. If ``None`` start at

adaptive.description: Use adaptive weights to combine the tapered spectra into PSD.

low_bias.description:

Maximum number of iterations to reach convergence when combining the
tapered spectra with adaptive weights (see argument ``adaptive``). This
argument has not effect if ``adaptive`` is set to ``False``.

verbose.description:
Control verbosity of the logging output. If ``None``, use the default

:func:`mne.verbose` for details. Should only be passed as a keyword

csd_array_multitaper.description: Estimate cross-spectral density from an array using a multitaper method.

t0.description:
Time of the first sample relative to the onset of the epoch, in
seconds. Defaults to 0.

tmin.description:
Minimum time instant to consider, in seconds. If ``None`` start at
first sample.

tmax.description:
Maximum time instant to consider, in seconds. If ``None`` end at last
sample.

ch_names.description:
A name for each time series. If ``None`` (the default), the series will
be named 'SERIES###'.

n_cycles.description:
Number of cycles to use when constructing Morlet wavelets. Fixed number
or one per frequency. Defaults to 7.

use_fft.description:
Whether to use FFT-based convolution to compute the wavelet transform.
Defaults to True.

n_jobs.description:
The number of jobs to run in parallel. If ``-1``, it is set
to the number of CPU cores. Requires the :mod:`joblib` package.
``None`` (default) is a marker for 'unset' that will be interpreted
as ``n_jobs=1`` (sequential execution) unless the call is performed under
a :class:`joblib:joblib.parallel_config` context manager that sets another
value for ``n_jobs``.

verbose.description:
Control verbosity of the logging output. If ``None``, use the default
verbosity level. See the :ref:`logging documentation <tut-logging>` and
:func:`mne.verbose` for details. Should only be passed as a keyword
argument.

csd_array_morlet.description: Estimate cross-spectral density from an array using Morlet wavelets.

sym.description:
Whether to generate a symmetric window (``True``, for filter design) or
a periodic window (``False``, for spectral analysis). Default is
``True``.

.. versionadded:: 1.3

dpss_windows.description:
Will give of orders [0,Kmax-1] for a given frequency-spacing multiple
NW and sequence length N.

.. note:: Copied from NiTime.

sigma.description:
It controls the width of the wavelet ie its temporal
resolution. If sigma is None the temporal resolution
is adapted with the frequency like for all wavelet transform.
The higher the frequency the shorter is the wavelet.
If sigma is fixed the temporal resolution is fixed
like for the short time Fourier transform and the number
of oscillations increases with the frequency.

zero_mean.description: Make sure the wavelet has a mean of zero.

morlet.description: Compute Morlet wavelets for the given frequency range.

tstep.description:
Step between successive windows in samples (must be a multiple of 2,
a divider of wsize and smaller than wsize/2) (default: wsize/2).

verbose.description:
Control verbosity of the logging output. If ``None``, use the default
verbosity level. See the :ref:`logging documentation <tut-logging>` and
:func:`mne.verbose` for details. Should only be passed as a keyword
argument.

stft.description:
The transformation is designed to be a tight frame that can be
perfectly inverted. It only returns the positive frequencies.

tstep.description:
Step between successive windows in samples (must be a multiple of 2,
a divider of wsize and smaller than wsize/2) (default: wsize/2).

Tx.description: Length of returned signal. If None Tx = n_step * tstep.

istft.description: ISTFT Inverse Short-Term Fourier Transform using a sine window.

sfreq.description:
Sampling frequency. If None the frequencies are given between 0 and pi
otherwise it's given in Hz.

fmin.description:
The lower- and upper-bound on frequencies of interest. Default is
``fmin=0, fmax=np.inf`` (spans all frequencies present in the data).

fmax.description:
The lower- and upper-bound on frequencies of interest. Default is
``fmin=0, fmax=np.inf`` (spans all frequencies present in the data).

bandwidth.description:
Frequency bandwidth of the multi-taper window function in Hz. For a
given frequency, frequencies at ``± bandwidth / 2`` are smoothed
together. The default value is a bandwidth of
``8 * (sfreq / n_times)``.

adaptive.description:
Use adaptive weights to combine the tapered spectra into PSD
(slow, use n_jobs >> 1 to speed up computation).

low_bias.description:
Only use tapers with more than 90% spectral concentration within
bandwidth.

normalization.description:
Normalization strategy. If "full", the PSD will be normalized by the
sampling rate as well as the length of the signal (as in
:ref:`Nitime <nitime:users-guide>`). Default is ``'length'``.

remove_dc.description:
If ``True``, the mean is subtracted from each segment before computing
its spectrum.

output.description:
The format of the returned ``psds`` array, ``'complex'`` or
``'power'``:

* ``'power'`` : the power spectral density is returned.
* ``'complex'`` : the complex fourier coefficients are returned per
  taper.

n_jobs.description:
The number of jobs to run in parallel. If ``-1``, it is set
to the number of CPU cores. Requires the :mod:`joblib` package.
``None`` (default) is a marker for 'unset' that will be interpreted
as ``n_jobs=1`` (sequential execution) unless the call is performed under
a :class:`joblib:joblib.parallel_config` context manager that sets another
value for ``n_jobs``.

max_iter.description:
Maximum number of iterations to reach convergence when combining the
tapered spectra with adaptive weights (see argument ``adaptive``). This
argument has not effect if ``adaptive`` is set to ``False``.

verbose.description:
Control verbosity of the logging output. If ``None``, use the default
verbosity level. See the :ref:`logging documentation <tut-logging>` and
:func:`mne.verbose` for details. Should only be passed as a keyword
argument.

psd_array_multitaper.description:
The power spectral density is computed with DPSS
tapers :footcite:p:`Slepian1978`.

n_fft.description:
The length of FFT used, must be ``>= n_per_seg`` (default: 256).
The segments will be zero-padded if ``n_fft > n_per_seg``.

n_overlap.description:
The number of points of overlap between segments. Will be adjusted
to be <= n_per_seg. The default value is 0.

n_per_seg.description:
Length of each Welch segment (windowed with a Hamming window). Defaults
to None, which sets n_per_seg equal to n_fft.

n_jobs.description:
The number of jobs to run in parallel. If ``-1``, it is set
to the number of CPU cores. Requires the :mod:`joblib` package.
``None`` (default) is a marker for 'unset' that will be interpreted
as ``n_jobs=1`` (sequential execution) unless the call is performed under
a :class:`joblib:joblib.parallel_config` context manager that sets another
value for ``n_jobs``.

average.description:
How to average the segments. If ``mean`` (default), calculate the
arithmetic mean. If ``median``, calculate the median, corrected for
its bias relative to the mean. If ``None``, returns the unaggregated
segments.

.. versionadded:: 0.19.0

window.description:
Windowing function to use. See :func:`scipy.signal.get_window`.

.. versionadded:: 0.22.0

remove_dc.description:
If ``True``, the mean is subtracted from each segment before computing
its spectrum.

output.description:
The format of the returned ``psds`` array, ``'complex'`` or
``'power'``:

* ``'power'`` : the power spectral density is returned.
* ``'complex'`` : the complex fourier coefficients are returned per
  window.

.. versionadded:: 1.4.0

verbose.description:
Control verbosity of the logging output. If ``None``, use the default
verbosity level. See the :ref:`logging documentation <tut-logging>` and
:func:`mne.verbose` for details. Should only be passed as a keyword
argument.

zero_mean.description:
If True, make sure the wavelets have a mean of zero. default False.

.. versionchanged:: 1.8
    The default will change from ``zero_mean=False`` in 1.6 to ``True`` in
    1.8.

use_fft.description: Use the FFT for convolutions or not. default True.

output.description:
* ``'complex'`` : single trial complex.
* ``'power'`` : single trial power.
* ``'phase'`` : single trial phase.
* ``'avg_power'`` : average of single trial power.
* ``'itc'`` : inter-trial coherence.
* ``'avg_power_itc'`` : average of single trial power and inter-trial
  coherence across trials.

n_jobs.description:
The number of jobs to run in parallel. If ``-1``, it is set
to the number of CPU cores. Requires the :mod:`joblib` package.
``None`` (default) is a marker for 'unset' that will be interpreted
as ``n_jobs=1`` (sequential execution) unless the call is performed under
a :class:`joblib:joblib.parallel_config` context manager that sets another
value for ``n_jobs``.
The number of epochs to process at the same time. The parallelization
is implemented across channels. Default 1.

verbose.description:
Control verbosity of the logging output. If ``None``, use the default
verbosity level. See the :ref:`logging documentation <tut-logging>` and
:func:`mne.verbose` for details. Should only be passed as a keyword
argument.

tfr_array_morlet.description:
Same computation as `~mne.time_frequency.tfr_morlet`, but operates on
:class:`NumPy arrays <numpy.ndarray>` instead of `~mne.Epochs` objects.

zero_mean.description: If True, make sure the wavelets have a mean of zero. Defaults to True.

use_fft.description: Use the FFT for convolutions or not. Defaults to True.

output.description:
* ``'complex'`` : single trial per taper complex values.
* ``'power'`` : single trial power.
* ``'phase'`` : single trial per taper phase.
* ``'avg_power'`` : average of single trial power.
* ``'itc'`` : inter-trial coherence.
* ``'avg_power_itc'`` : average of single trial power and inter-trial
  coherence across trials.

n_jobs.description:
The number of jobs to run in parallel. If ``-1``, it is set
to the number of CPU cores. Requires the :mod:`joblib` package.
``None`` (default) is a marker for 'unset' that will be interpreted
as ``n_jobs=1`` (sequential execution) unless the call is performed under
a :class:`joblib:joblib.parallel_config` context manager that sets another
value for ``n_jobs``.
The parallelization is implemented across channels.

return_weights.description:
If True, return the taper weights. Only applies if ``output='complex'`` or
``'phase'``.

.. versionadded:: 1.10.0

verbose.description:
Control verbosity of the logging output. If ``None``, use the default
verbosity level. See the :ref:`logging documentation <tut-logging>` and
:func:`mne.verbose` for details. Should only be passed as a keyword
argument.

tfr_array_multitaper.description:
Same computation as `~mne.time_frequency.tfr_multitaper`, but operates on
:class:`NumPy arrays <numpy.ndarray>` instead of `~mne.Epochs` or
`~mne.Evoked` objects.

fmin.description:
The minimum frequency to include. If None defaults to the minimum fft
frequency greater than zero.

fmax.description: The maximum frequency to include. If None defaults to the maximum fft.

n_fft.description:
The length of the windows used for FFT. If None, it defaults to the
next power of 2 larger than the signal length.

width.description:
The width of the Gaussian window. If < 1, increased temporal
resolution, if > 1, increased frequency resolution. Defaults to 1.
(classical S-Transform).

return_itc.description: Return intertrial coherence (ITC) as well as averaged power.

n_jobs.description:
The number of jobs to run in parallel. If ``-1``, it is set
to the number of CPU cores. Requires the :mod:`joblib` package.
``None`` (default) is a marker for 'unset' that will be interpreted
as ``n_jobs=1`` (sequential execution) unless the call is performed under
a :class:`joblib:joblib.parallel_config` context manager that sets another
value for ``n_jobs``.

verbose.description:
Control verbosity of the logging output. If ``None``, use the default
verbosity level. See the :ref:`logging documentation <tut-logging>` and
:func:`mne.verbose` for details. Should only be passed as a keyword
argument.

tfr_array_stockwell.description:
Same computation as `~mne.time_frequency.tfr_stockwell`, but operates on
:class:`NumPy arrays <numpy.ndarray>` instead of `~mne.Epochs` objects.

See :footcite:`Stockwell2007,MoukademEtAl2014,WheatEtAl2010,JonesEtAl2006`
for more information.

use_fft.description: Use FFT for convolutions. Defaults to True.

mode.description:
Convention for convolution. 'full' is currently not implemented with
``use_fft=False``. Defaults to ``'same'``.

cwt.description: Compute time-frequency decomposition with continuous wavelet transform.

hemi.description:
Hemisphere id (ie 'lh', 'rh', 'both', or 'split'). In the case
of 'both', both hemispheres are shown in the same window.
In the case of 'split' hemispheres are displayed side-by-side
in different viewing panes.

title.description: Title for the window.

cortex.description:
Specifies how the cortical surface is rendered. Options:

1. The name of one of the preset cortex styles:
    ``'classic'`` (default), ``'high_contrast'``,
    ``'low_contrast'``, or ``'bone'``.
2. A single color-like argument to render the cortex as a single
    color, e.g. ``'red'`` or ``(0.1, 0.4, 1.)``.
3. A list of two color-like used to render binarized curvature
    values for gyral (first) and sulcal (second). regions, e.g.,
    ``['red', 'blue']`` or ``[(1, 0, 0), (0, 0, 1)]``.
4. A dict containing keys ``'vmin', 'vmax', 'colormap'`` with
    values used to render the binarized curvature (where 0 is gyral,
    1 is sulcal).

.. versionchanged:: 0.24
   Add support for non-string arguments.

subjects_dir.description:
If not None, this directory will be used as the subjects directory
instead of the value set using the SUBJECTS_DIR environment
variable.

views.description:
View to use. Using multiple views (list) is not supported for mpl
backend. See :meth:`Brain.show_view <mne.viz.Brain.show_view>` for
valid string options.

offset.description:
If True, shifts the right- or left-most x coordinate of the left and
right surfaces, respectively, to be at zero. This is useful for viewing
inflated surface where hemispheres typically overlap. Can be "auto"
(default) use True with inflated surfaces and False otherwise
(Default: 'auto'). Only used when ``hemi='both'``.

.. versionchanged:: 0.23
   Default changed to "auto".

interaction.description:
Can be "trackball" (default) or "terrain", i.e. a turntable-style
camera.

view_layout.description:
Can be "vertical" (default) or "horizontal". When using "horizontal" mode,
the PyVista backend must be used and hemi cannot be "split".

silhouette.description:
As a dict, it contains the ``color``, ``linewidth``, ``alpha`` opacity
and ``decimate`` (level of decimation between 0 and 1 or None) of the
brain's silhouette to display. If True, the default values are used
and if False, no silhouette will be displayed. Defaults to False.

show.description: Display the window as soon as it is ready. Defaults to True.

Brain.description:
.. warning::
   The API for this class is not currently complete. We suggest using
   :meth:`mne.viz.plot_source_estimates` with the PyVista backend
   enabled to obtain a ``Brain`` instance.

ClickableImage.description:
Takes as input an image array (can be any array that works with imshow,
but will work best with images.  Displays the image and lets you
click on it.  Stores the xy coordinates of each click, so now you can
superimpose something on top of it.

Upon clicking, the x/y coordinate of the cursor will be stored in
self.coords, which is a list of (x, y) tuples.

time.description:
The time point at which the field map shall be displayed. If None,
the average peak latency (across sensor types) is used.

time_label.description: How to print info about the time instant visualized.

n_jobs.description:
The number of jobs to run in parallel. If ``-1``, it is set
to the number of CPU cores. Requires the :mod:`joblib` package.
``None`` (default) is a marker for 'unset' that will be interpreted
as ``n_jobs=1`` (sequential execution) unless the call is performed under
a :class:`joblib:joblib.parallel_config` context manager that sets another
value for ``n_jobs``.

vmax.description:
Maximum intensity. Can be a dictionary with two entries ``"eeg"`` and ``"meg"``
to specify separate values for EEG and MEG fields respectively. Can be
``None`` to use the maximum value of the data.

.. versionadded:: 0.21
.. versionadded:: 1.4
    ``vmax`` can be a dictionary to specify separate values for EEG and
    MEG fields.

show_density.description:
Whether to draw the field density as an overlay on top of the helmet/head
surface. Defaults to ``True``.

alpha.description:
Opacity of the meshes (between 0 and 1). Can be a dictionary with two
entries ``"eeg"`` and ``"meg"`` to specify separate values for EEG and
MEG fields respectively. Can be ``None`` to use 1.0 when a single field
map is shown, or ``dict(eeg=1.0, meg=0.5)`` when both field maps are shown.

.. versionadded:: 1.4

interpolation.description:
Interpolation method (:class:`scipy.interpolate.interp1d` parameter).
Must be one of ``'linear'``, ``'nearest'``, ``'zero'``, ``'slinear'``,
``'quadratic'`` or ``'cubic'``.

.. versionadded:: 1.6

interaction.description:
How interactions with the scene via an input device (e.g., mouse or
trackpad) modify the camera position. If ``'terrain'``, one axis is
fixed, enabling "turntable-style" rotations. If ``'trackball'``,
movement along all axes is possible, which provides more freedom of
movement, but you may incidentally perform unintentional rotations along
some axes.
Defaults to ``'terrain'``.

.. versionadded:: 1.1

time_viewer.description:
Display time viewer GUI. Can also be ``"auto"``, which will mean
``True`` if there is more than one time point and ``False`` otherwise.

.. versionadded:: 1.6

verbose.description:
Control verbosity of the logging output. If ``None``, use the default
verbosity level. See the :ref:`logging documentation <tut-logging>` and
:func:`mne.verbose` for details. Should only be passed as a keyword
argument.

EvokedField.description: Plot MEG/EEG fields on head surface and helmet in 3D.

set_ratios.description:
Set the aspect ratio of any axes in fig
to the value in set_ratios. Defaults to None,
which does nothing to axes.

add_background_image.description:
Adds the image specified in ``im`` to the
figure ``fig``. This is generally meant to
be done with topo plots, though it could work
for any plot.

.. note:: This modifies the figure and/or axes in place.

show.description: If True, show the resulting diff in a new tab in a web browser.

read_limit.description:
Max number of bytes of data to read from a tag. Can be np.inf
to always read all data (helps test read completion).

max_str.description:
Max number of characters of string representation to print for
each tag's data.

verbose.description:
Control verbosity of the logging output. If ``None``, use the default
verbosity level. See the :ref:`logging documentation <tut-logging>` and
:func:`mne.verbose` for details. Should only be passed as a keyword
argument.

compare_fiff.description: Compare the contents of two fiff files using diff and show_fiff.

start_pos.description: Angle in degrees that defines where the first node is plotted.

start_between.description:
If True, the layout starts with the position between the nodes. This is
the same as adding "180. / len(node_names)" to start_pos.

group_sep.description: Group separation angle in degrees. See "group_boundaries".

circular_layout.description: Create layout arranging nodes on a circle.

layout_scale.description:
Scaling factor for adjusting the relative size of the layout
on the canvas. If None, nothing will be scaled.

legend.description:
If True, an additional axis is created in the bottom right corner
that can be used to, e.g., construct a legend. The index of this
axis will be -1.

select.description:
Whether to enable the lasso-selection tool to enable the user to select
channels. The selected channels will be available in
``fig.lasso.selection``.

.. versionadded:: 1.10.0

iter_topography.description:
This function returns a generator that unpacks into
a series of matplotlib axis objects and data / channel
indices, both corresponding to the sensor positions
of the related layout passed or inferred from the channel info.
Hence, this enables convenient topography plot customization.

format.description:
Type of colormap to return. If 'matplotlib', will return a
matplotlib.colors.LinearSegmentedColormap. If 'vtk', will
return an RGBA array of shape (256, 4).

mne_analyze_colormap.description: Return a colormap similar to that used by mne_analyze.

slices.description:
The indices of the MRI slices to plot. If ``None``, automatically
pick 12 equally-spaced slices.

brain_surfaces.description:
One or more brain surface to plot (optional). Entries should correspond
to files in the subject's ``surf`` directory (e.g. ``"white"``).

show_indices.description:
Show slice indices if True.

.. versionadded:: 0.20

mri.description:
